subject: science
chapter_title: Exploring the Investigative World of Science

primary.justification: This is an orientation chapter whose architectural subject is the practice of scientific inquiry itself. The extended puri investigation worked example (pages 6–7) explicitly teaches students to formulate focused scientific questions, identify controllable vs observable variables, design controlled experiments (change one variable at a time), and record observations systematically — the exact cognitive operations C-6.2 specifies. No CG-1 through CG-5 content domain is the primary subject; the chapter is an epistemological and methodological orientation, not a content chapter.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to conduct systematic scientific inquiry by learning to formulate focused questions, identify controllable and observable variables, design simple controlled experiments, and iterate investigations based on observations.
